mission: Derive the strongest symmetry-level source map for the remaining Paper 35 scalar frontier.

theorem_1_scalar_data_completeness.statement: After the current-stack reductions, every remaining scalar datum relevant to the baryogenesis source map belongs to the list {(M1, m_tilde1), Q5, theta(R)}. No additional operator-class data remain.
remaining_scalar_data: CP-odd boundary scalar: Q5 = N_+ - N_-
support-side scalar production history: lambda(R), equivalently theta(R)

theorem_2_cp_parity_factorization.statement: The visible precursor Y_(B-L) is CP-odd. The residual data M1, m_tilde1, and theta(R) are CP-even scalars, while Q5 is the unique residual CP-odd scalar. Therefore any admissible source map must factor through Q5 and vanish when Q5 = 0.

theorem_3_current_stack_zero_source.statement: Because the current stack gives Q5 = 0, the source asymmetry vanishes identically on the current stack irrespective of the unresolved support-side scalar production history theta(R) and irrespective of the unresolved CP-even magnitude selector.

final_boundary.safe_statement: The dominant remaining Paper 35 bottleneck is now exact: without a theorem generating nonzero Q5, baryogenesis is impossible on the current stack even if the support-side scalar production law theta(R) were supplied.
still_needed_for_positive_closure: a theorem deriving nonzero Q5 on the boundary sector
a theorem selecting the CP-even magnitude pair (M1, m_tilde1)
a theorem deriving the support-side scalar production history theta(R)
